sourceMovie.title: Memoirs of a Geisha
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Memoirs of a Geisha (2005) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Anna Karenina
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Memoirs of a Geisha clicked for you, Anna Karenina (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Memoirs of a Geisha?
faq.answer: Try Anna Karenina, Hamnet, Anna and the King—each shares DNA with Memoirs of a Geisha while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Memoirs of a Geisha?
faq.answer: Memoirs of a Geisha (2005) is best described as Drama, Romance, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Memoirs of a Geisha worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
